ground_truth_source: /Volumes/transit/Games/1/ (Sporting AC — cross-game test, same calibration as Run 45)

baseline.notes: Original sporting_ac batch run (5 frames, no YOLO crop): F1 0.153, P 0.733, R 0.085, TP 11/129. Coarse VLM firing rate 3.1% (22/672). This is the game where the pixel-per-player bottleneck is most severe. Run 45 with 7 frames nudged F1 to 0.208 but coarse firing rate didn't move (3.1%), rejecting density as the lever. Run 47 tests YOLO tight-crop: if pixel density IS the structural bottleneck, coarse firing rate should climb substantially from 3.1%, closing the gap with Rush's 22.8%.

targets.notes: Two signals to evaluate. Primary diagnostic: coarse firing rate. If it doesn't move from 3.1%, the hypothesis is rejected regardless of F1 (crop didn't expose anything new to the VLM). If firing rate jumps to >=10%, the hypothesis is supported. Secondary: F1. A jump from 0.153 to 0.30+ would be a strong cross-game win. If firing rate moves but F1 barely moves, extra fires are FPs (like Run 46's prompt change); revisit. If both move, proceed to Run 48 on Rush for regression check, then batch all 4 games.

yolo_crop_enabled.reason: Run 47 feature — YOLO tight-crop before VLM. Smoke test showed dramatic field-focus effect on sporting_ac (sky/mountains/parking removed, players ~2x larger).

notes: Run #47 — diagnostic FIRST on sporting_ac (inverted from initial Rush-first plan). The hypothesis is that pixel density is the cross-game bottleneck; sporting_ac is where that bottleneck is most visible (3.1% vs Rush's 22.8% firing). Running the test on the game with the clearest failure mode gives the highest signal-to-noise for validating or rejecting the hypothesis. Rush regression check comes AFTER the hypothesis is validated. Commit c230b26 (same as killed Rush submission). Worker restarted on afebe70 @ 23:29:07Z.
